title: A diagonal two-dimensional operator maps zero to zero

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: A diagonal two-dimensional linear operator sends the zero vector to the zero vector.
assumptions: a and b are real diagonal coefficients
informal.expected_conclusion: Both diagonal output coordinates are zero.

declarations.name: diag2First
declarations.statement: def diag2First (a x : Real) : Real := a * x

declarations.name: diag2Second
declarations.statement: def diag2Second (b y : Real) : Real := b * y

declarations.name: diag2_zero
declarations.statement: theorem diag2_zero (a b : Real) : diag2First a 0 = 0 /\ diag2Second b 0 = 0

tasks.input: Translate the zero-vector property for a diagonal two-dimensional operator into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem diag2_zero (a b : Real) : diag2First a 0 = 0 /\ diag2Second b 0 = 0

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof by unfolding the diagonal coordinate definitions and simplifying.
tasks.target: Split the conjunction, unfold each coordinate, and use ring.
